holdings.name: HDFC Bank
holdings.description: India's largest private bank with 8,000+ branches, 25M+ customers and ~4% NIM. Largest constituent of Nifty 50, Sensex and virtually all Indian large-cap indices. Dominant in retail mortgages, credit cards and SME banking post HDFC Ltd merger.

holdings.name: Reliance Industries
holdings.description: India's largest company by revenue. Jio Platforms (450M+ subscribers), Reliance Retail (India's largest retailer) and Jamnagar refinery-petrochemical complex (world's largest single-site). New Energy: 100 GW solar plan and battery gigafactory underway.

holdings.name: ICICI Bank
holdings.description: India's 2nd largest private bank, ROE ~18%, NIM 4.3%. iMobile app with 40M+ users leads digital banking. Diversified across retail, corporate and SME segments. Asset quality best-in-class with PCR >80%.

holdings.name: Infosys
holdings.description: India's 2nd largest IT firm with $18B+ revenue and 300,000+ employees. AI-led services (Topaz platform), Cloud migration and ERP modernisation for global Fortune 500. Steady 20-25% dividend payout ratio.

holdings.name: DLF Limited
holdings.description: India's largest real estate developer. ₹13,000+ Cr pre-sales in FY25 driven by luxury residences. DLF Cyber City (Gurugram) dominates Grade-A office leasing. Net debt largely cleared; entering a high-FCF phase.

holdings.name: LIC of India
holdings.description: World's largest insurer by policy count with 300M+ policies. India's largest institutional investor managing ₹45 lakh crore AUM. Benefiting from financialisation of savings — insurance penetration rising from 3.2% to target 5%.
